pipeline.description: Classifies the issue as bug, feature, chore, or spike. Validates required fields (acceptance criteria, stakeholder). Rejects malformed issues with a correction comment.
Produces a Product Requirements Document as a comment. Covers problem statement, user stories, success metrics, and out-of-scope items. Blocks on human approval.
Cross-references the PRD against all active product-layer standards. Flags violations before design begins, with STD ID and rationale.
Analyses the blast radius of the proposed change. Identifies affected layers, services, files, and data models. Flags if scope crosses more than one bounded context.
Identifies external dependencies: open tickets that must complete first, third-party APIs, required data, and team dependencies. Blocks progression on unresolved hard dependencies.

Assesses whether the ticket fits a single development cycle. Applies S/M/L/XL sizing. If XL, mandates decomposition into child tickets before design work begins. Each child re-enters the pipeline from issue-classifier.
Produces a technical design covering data model changes, API contracts, component boundaries, integration points, and non-functional requirements. Flags ADR candidates.
Reviews the technical design for decisions warranting an ADR. Drafts ADR stubs in the correct JSON schema format. Non-blocking if no ADRs are needed.
Generates a testing specification from PRD acceptance criteria and technical design. Produces numbered Gherkin scenarios (SC-NNN). Distinguishes unit, integration, and E2E scope.
Validates the test spec for completeness. Every PRD acceptance criterion must map to a Gherkin scenario. Every API endpoint or data mutation must have a test case. Blocks on gaps.
Breaks the parent issue into ordered child implementation task issues. Each task is scoped to a single file or concern, carries a done condition, and references applicable STD IDs.
Analyses child task dependencies, identifies the critical path, and produces a sequenced build order. Flags tasks that can run in parallel.
Implements each child task in dependency order. Opens one PR per task prefixed [coder]. Follows applicable STDs. Self-reviews against the done condition and linked Gherkin scenario.
Reviews changed files against active architecture standards. Raises issues prefixed [standards-compliance-reviewer] for violations with rationale and proposed diffs for simple fixes.
Validates SQL migration files against database-layer standards. Confirms forward-only, idempotent migrations. Blocks merge on naming, RLS, or type violations.
Reviews the PR against the technical design and test spec. Checks scope creep, confirms implementation matches architecture decisions, and verifies all STD violations are resolved.
Implements tests from the testing spec. Names each test to its Gherkin scenario ID (SC-NNN). Follows testing-layer standards.
Runs the full test suite. Posts coverage delta, pass/fail summary, and failing scenario IDs as a PR comment. Blocks merge on failures or coverage regression.
Compares coverage against test spec thresholds. Flags acceptance criterion scenarios without a passing test. Blocks merge if coverage drops or any required scenario is uncovered.
Generates a changelog entry from the PRD and merged PR. Opens a follow-up PR to add it to CHANGELOG. Closes all child task issues and posts a completion summary on the parent issue.
Produces a structured retrospective: phase durations, standards violations raised, test spec completeness at build start, PRD changes post-design, and improvement suggestions.
Reviews closed retrospectives and recurring violation patterns. Drafts new or updated standards in JSON schema format and opens an issue for human review and approval.
